coredata.dc:title: Development of a sensitive electrochemical immunosensor using polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots for detecting a depression marker
coredata.dc:creator: Sun, Bolu
Wang, Yanping
Li, Dai
Li, Wuyan
Gou, Xiaodan
Gou, Yuqiang
Hu, Fangdi
coredata.dc:description: As a new type of conductive material, polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD), which were prepared by in-situ polymerization had been used to construct a novel electrochemical immunosensor for early screening of depression markers-heat shock protein 70 (HSP70). Profiting from the huge specific surface area, good bioactivity and excellent structure of PAGD, a variety of heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) was firmly loaded on the surface of PAGD for successful construction of basic electrode (HSP70/PAGD/GCE), which was characterized by cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS), respectively. Due to the HSP70 fixed on the surface of basic electrode and the HSP70 in the samples can competitively combine with the horseradish peroxidase labeled human HSP70 antibody (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab). As a result, there is negative correlation between the concentration of HSP70 in samples and the detection signal of the proposed electrochemical immunosensor (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE) in the test liquid. Under conditions optimized for determining HSP70, wide linearity was obtained in the range of 0.0976–100 ng/mL, with a low detection limit of 0.05 ng/mL at 3σ. Moreover, the proposed electrochemical immunosensors was successfully applied to detect HSP70 in plasma samples, and exhibited good precision, acceptable stability and reproducibility. Therefore, this study provides a novel and convenient method for early clinical screening of depression markers, and also provides a reliable and objective analysis method for the diagnosis of depression at the molecular level.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Polyaniline
Graphene quantum dots
Immunosensor
Depression markers

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Preparation of polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD)
Preparation of polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD)
Electrochemical characterization of the prepared sensor
Reproducibility and stability of the immunosensor
Application of the immunosensor in human serum

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Key Research and Development Program of China Stem Cell and Translational Research
National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM images of the (A) GQDs, (B) PANI, (C) PAGD. (D) FT-IR spectra of GO(a), GQDs(b), PANI(c) and PAGD(d). (E) Raman spectra of GO (a), GQDs (b), PANI(c) and PAGD (d).

(A, B) EIS of Bare GCE (a), GQDs/GCE (b), PANI/GCE (c), PAGD/GCE (d) and HSP70/PAGD/GCE (e) in 0.5 mM (Fe(CN)6)3−/4− (0.1 M KCl) solution. Inset: Randles equivalent circuitused to model impedance data in the presence of redox couples. (C) CVs of Bare GCE (a), GQDs/GCE (b), PANI/GCE (c), PAGD/GCE (d) and HSP70/PAGD/GCE (e) in 0.5 mM (Fe(CN)6)3−/4− (0.1 M KCl) solution. Scan rate: 100 mV/s. (D) CVs of PAGD/GCE (a), HSP70/PAGD/GCE (b) and HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE (c) in 0.1 M pH 7.4 PBS containing 2 mM o-PD and 4 mM H2O2.

(A) The relationship between OD values and the concentration of HSP70 at 450 nm. (B) Calibration curve of the ELISA for HSP70 determination plotted on a semi-log scale. (C) The electrochemical response of immunosensor (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE with increasing HSP70) for detecting HSP70 with different concentration in 0.1 M pH 7.4 PBS containing 2 mM o-PD and 4 mM H2O2. The differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) conditions were as follows: scan range, −0.5 ~ −0.8 V; increment potential, 0.004 V; pulse amplitude, 0.05 V; pulse width, 0.05 s. (D) The relationship between DPV response values of immunosensor and the concentration of HSP70. (E) Calibration curve of the immunosensor for cortisol determination plotted on a semi-log scale.
The specificity of the developed immunosensing probe toward HSP110, HSP90, HSP60 and HSP28.

Schematic display the synthesis for polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) (A) and fabrication of electrochemical immunosensor for determination of Depression markers- heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) (B).
Comparison of the proposed immunosensor with previous works.

head.ce:title: Development of a sensitive electrochemical immunosensor using polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots for detecting a depression marker

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Pharmacy, Lanzhou University, Lanzhou 730000, China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Pharmacy, Lanzhou University, Lanzhou 730000, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: b
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Center for Inflammation, Translational and Clinical Lung Research, Temple University School of Medicine, Philadelphia, PA, USA

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Center for Inflammation, Translational and Clinical Lung Research

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Center for Inflammation, Translational and Clinical Lung Research, Temple University School of Medicine, Philadelphia, PA, USA

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Nanjing University, 210046, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Nanjing University, 210046, China

ce:affiliation.ce:label: d
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Center for Disease Prevention and Control in Northwest Theater of the Chinese People's Liberation Army, Lanzhou 730000, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Center for Disease Prevention and Control in Northwest Theater of the Chinese People's Liberation Army

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Center for Disease Prevention and Control in Northwest Theater of the Chinese People's Liberation Army, Lanzhou 730000, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: As a new type of conductive material, polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD), which were prepared by in-situ polymerization had been used to construct a novel electrochemical immunosensor for early screening of depression markers-heat shock protein 70 (HSP70). Profiting from the huge specific surface area, good bioactivity and excellent structure of PAGD, a variety of heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) was firmly loaded on the surface of PAGD for successful construction of basic electrode (HSP70/PAGD/GCE), which was characterized by cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS), respectively. Due to the HSP70 fixed on the surface of basic electrode and the HSP70 in the samples can competitively combine with the horseradish peroxidase labeled human HSP70 antibody (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab). As a result, there is negative correlation between the concentration of HSP70 in samples and the detection signal of the proposed electrochemical immunosensor (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE) in the test liquid. Under conditions optimized for determining HSP70, wide linearity was obtained in the range of 0.0976–100 ng/mL, with a low detection limit of 0.05 ng/mL at 3σ. Moreover, the proposed electrochemical immunosensors was successfully applied to detect HSP70 in plasma samples, and exhibited good precision, acceptable stability and reproducibility. Therefore, this study provides a novel and convenient method for early clinical screening of depression markers, and also provides a reliable and objective analysis method for the diagnosis of depression at the molecular level.

ce:section.ce:para: Depression is a mental illness with characters of low mood, thinking retardation and thinking volitional activity drops [1]. According to the prediction, depression will become the second critical disease following to the cardiovascular disease around the world in 2020 [2]. How early screening and accurate diagnosis of depression will be realized has become a vital problem in the treatment of depression [3]. At present, scale evaluation and clinical judgment which are the general methods for clinical diagnosis of depression, have obvious subjectivity and are prone to misdiagnosis and misjudgement. There is a lack of accurately and reliably diagnostic tools and objective standards for depression. Investigating and developing a method for the analysis and detection of important molecular markers which related to depression will expect to provide effective means for the objective and accurate diagnosis of depression.

Recently, with the continuous development of life science, many investigations have manifested that the cause of depression is closely related to stress which could affect the central nervous system. Hippocampus is the main brain region modulating stress response in the central nervous system. In addition, it is the damaged area of the brain when the body is in a state of stress. Heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) can be produced as a protective protein during stress. In normal conditions, the concentration of HSP70 is low in the central and peripheral nervous system, it will significantly increase to protect the cell from being damaged when the body is in a state of stress [4]. During the stress, the oxidized low-density lipoprotein (OxLDL) and homocysteine (HCY) as the stressors will affect vascular endothelial cell and induce heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) to release from the inside to the outside of the cell. Finally, the level of plasma HSP70 will be increased significantly. That phenomenon has been regard as the danger signal of stress stimulation. Therefore, HSP70 could be seen as the ultra-sensitive marker during the process in which brain tissue cells were reversibly damaged. Besides, the plasma HSP70 presents an obvious correlation with stress condition. As a result, the HSP70 has been associated with the development of depression, which can be considered as a precursory and diagnostic marker for depression [5].

How the HSP70 was sensitively, accurately and rapidly analyzed has become a hot spot, which has been studied and concerned by many analysts. Currently accepted methods for the detection of HSP70 include Western blot [6], flow cytometry (FCM) [7], immunohistochemical staining [8,9] and immunoblotting [10]. Although these methods have realized the determination of heat shock protein 70 (HSP70), while the feature of low sensitivity, longer time for analysis and complex experimental process belong to these methods limited their using. New methods are needed to fleetly detect HSP70 with volatile concentration level. In recent years, the electrochemical immunoassay has attracted extensive attention due to its unique advantages such as high sensitivity, low cost and rapid analysis [11–14], it has also been widely used in sensitive detection of many disease-related proteins [15,16]. Wei and his co-workers [17] developed an ultrasensitive electrochemical immunosensor for the detection of ochratoxin A based on Au octahedron plasmonic colloidosomes. The proposed immunosensor obtained a wide linear range and a low detection limit. Afkhami et al. [18] prepared a novel nanocomposite consisting of electrosynthesized gold nanodendrites and chitosan nanoparticles (AuNDs/CSNPs) and it was used to fabricate an impedimetric immunosensor based on a screen printed carbon electrode (SPCE) for the rapid and sensitive immunoassay of botulinum neurotoxin A (BoNT/A). The practical applicability of the proposed sensor was examined by evaluating the detection of BoNT/A in milk and serum samples with satisfactory recoveries. It can be seen that electrochemical immunoassay has unique features in covering the insufficient shortage of sensitive detection for disease markers [19–24]. Therefore, developing an immunosensor for sensitive, efficient and real-time determination of HSP70, which is used to resolve the insufficiency of recent methods for determination of HSP70, has become an important science and technology problem that must be solved.

However, the performance of electrochemical sensor is mainly depended on the properties of electrode-modified material. Graphene quantum dots (GQDs), a kind of graphene-layered material with the size less than 100 nm [25], not only inherits the highly electrical conductivity and high stability, but also has the significant quantum confinement and edge effects, which gave GQDs a variety of new physical and chemical properties [26,27], such as good electron transfer rate and chemical stability. Therefore, GQDs can be used as an ideal sensor material [28,29]. Roushani et al. [30] constructed a novel electrochemical sensor for the rapid detection of persulfate using graphene quantum dots/riboflavin nanocomposite. Benefiting from the excellent catalytic activity of nanocomposite, the persulfate (S2O8 2−) was sensitively analyzed by the proposed sensor. This study put forward a new way to develop electrochemical sensors with the usage of graphene quantum dots.

Polyaniline (PANI) is a rising star in conductive polymers, which has attracted great attention because of the special proton doping, good environmental stability, excellent conductivity, high-yield and low cost [31]. The composite prepared with PANI and inorganic conductive nanomaterial not only has both the superior performance, but also possesses other new performance, which has made its composite useful for application in solar cells [32], super-capacitors [33], and analytical sensors [34,35].

For the above considerations, polyaniline (PANI) and graphene quantum dots (GQDs) were used to prepare polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) by in-situ polymerization as new sensor material (Scheme 1A). Polyaniline (PANI) effectively prevented the aggregation of graphene quantum dots (GQDs) and increased the specific surface of the composite. When polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) with good conductivity was applied to construct immunosensors, they presented high selectivity and sensitivity, and achieved the sensitive determination of HSP70 in real samples with satisfactory results. Besides, the proposed electrochemical immunosensor also exhibited good precision, acceptable stability and reproducibility (Scheme 1B). Therefore, the present work offered a novel and convenient way for early screening of depression marker-heat shock protein 70.

Graphene oxide was supplied by XFNANO technology Co., Ltd. (China). Ferric trichloride was purchased from ALFA (China). Aniline was purchased from Xi'an Chemical Reagent Factory. Bovine serum albumin (BSA) and Tween-20, heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) kits, heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) (Ag), biotinylated HSP70 monoclonal antibody (Biotin-Ab) and horse radish peroxidase labeled streptavidin (HRP–Strept) were provided by Elabscience Biotechnology Co., Ltd. (Wuhan, China). 30% H2O2 and 99% o-phenylenediamine (o-PD) were obtained from Shanghai reagent Co. Inc. (Shanghai, China). Phosphate buffer solution (PBS), Phosphate buffered solution containing 0.05% Tween-20 (PBST). All other reagents were of analytical grade and all aqueous solutions were prepared using distilled water.

Electrochemical measurements including cyclic voltammetry (CV), electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) were all performed on a CHI 6041E workstation (Shanghai Chenhua, China) with a conventional three electrode system. Scanning electron microscopic (SEM) images were obtained using JSM-6701F Cold field emission type sweeping mirror (Japan electronics Optics Corporation). The Raman spectrometer (Beijing Zolix Instrument, China) and Nicolet IS5 infrared spectrometer (Thermo, US) were used to characterize the prepared materimal. Bio-Rad 680 microplate reader was used in HSP70 ELISA reaction.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD)

ce:section.ce:para: A simple sono-Fenton method [36] was used for synthesis of GQDs. In a typical procedure of GQDs preparation, 40 mg Graphene oxide (GO) was put into 50 mL distilled water and sonicated for 2.5 h. Subsequently, 20 mL of 30% H2O2 was added drop by drop into the above solution under constant stirring. Afterwards, 10 mL FeCl3 (2 mg/mL) was dispersed with stirring. The obtained mixture was sonicated in an ultrasonic cleaner for 8 h. Finally, the aqueous solution of GQDs was lyophilized to obtain the GQDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD)
ce:section.ce:para: Initially, 20 mg GQDs and 200 μL aniline were added into 20 mL water and the solution was stirred for 1.5 h at 10–12 °C. Then, 10 mL ammonium persulfate (APS, 5% w/v) was added and stirred to mixture solution which was left overnight at 5 °C. The APS and oligoaniline of the product was filtered and washed repeatedly by water and methanol. Finally, the prepared polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD) was successfully obtained [37] and naturally dried. The dry PAGD was dispersed in distilled water through ultra-sonication to get 2 mg/mL solution for follow-up experiments.

The immunoassay procedure was illustrated in Scheme 1B. Before the modification, the bare glassy carbon electrode (GCE) was successively polished with 0.3 and 0.05 μm gamma alumina powder (γ-Al2O3) on a polishing cloth, and then ultrasonically rinsed by ethanol and distilled water. After natural drying, 4 μL polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD) (2 mg/mL) was cast on the surface of GCE electrode and dried to obtain PAGD/GCE electrode. Then the PAGD/GCE electrode was immersed in 50 μL fresh heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) solution (50 ng/mL with pH 7.4) at 4 °C overnight to obtain the HSP70/PAGD/GCE basic electrode. Next, the un-immobilized HSP70 was removed with PBST (PBS, 0.05% Tween-20). Also, it was incubated into 50 μL 2% bovine serum albumin (BSA) solution containing PBST (PBS, 0.05% Tween-20) for 50 min at 37 °C to block possible remaining active sites with nonspecific binding. The fabricated basic electrode was rinsed with PBST (PBS, 0.05% Tween-20) and stored at 4 °C without using.

The electrochemical properties of the proposed basic electrode was investigated by cyclic voltammetry (CV) and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) in the 5.0 mM K3[Fe(CN)6]/K4[Fe(CN)6] containing 0.1 M KCl. The EIS measurements were studied at amplitude of 0.005 V and potential of 0.22 V with a frequency range from 0. 1 to 105 Hz, CV studies were performed by scanning from −0.2 to 0.5 V at a scan rate of 100 mV/s.

Electrochemical measurement of electrochemical immunosensor was employed in 5.0 mL test liquid (pH 7.0 PBS containing 2.0 mM o-PD and 4.0 mM H2O2) by scanning from −0.8 to −0.3 V at a scan rate of 100 mV/s. Nitrogen was bubbled into the test liquid to remove the dissolved oxygen before the characterization.

The first step involved incubation in 50 μL of biotin HSP70 antibody diluent (Biotin-Ag, The biotin HSP70 antibody diluent was prepared based on the HSP70 ELISA Kit product description, Catalog No: E-EL-H1863c) containing different concentrations of standard HSP70 solution (The standard HSP70 solution concentrations are as follows: 100, 50, 25, 12.5, 6.25, 3.125, 1.5625, 0.781, 0.3905, 0.19525, 0.0976, 0.0488, 0.0244, 0 ng/mL, which were prepared in 0.1 M PBS, pH 7.4) or the samples (The clinical serum samples were kindly provided by the First Hospital of Lanzhou University (Lanzhou, China)) onto the HSP70/PAGD/GCE for 60 min at 37 °C. At this temperature, the immunoreaction can get higher efficiency. The non-specifically bounded Biotin-Ab was washed using PBST (PBS, 0.05% Tween-20), and getting HSP70/PG/GCE electrodes modified by different amount of Biotin-Ab (Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE). After a washing step, 50 μL horse radish peroxidase conjugate streptavidin (HRP-Strept) was dropped onto the resulting Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrodes and allowed incubation for 30 min at 37 °C. The phosphate buffered solution containing Tween-20 (PBS, 0.05% Tween-20) also was used to wash the non-specifically bounded HRP-Strept. Finally, the electrochemical immunosensor (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE) was fabricated. The following amperometric measurements were operated by immersion of the immunosensor into 5.0 mL pH 7.4 PBS containing 2.0 mM o-PD and 4.0 mM H2O2 solution for 8 min. The differential pulse voltammograms (DPV) scan was used to evaluate the sensitivity of the fabricated electrochemical immunosensor and response of current for different concentrations of standard HSP70 solution or the samples at the potential range of −0.5 ~ −0.8 V. Nitrogen was bubbled into the test liquid to remove the dissolved oxygen before the determination. Finally, the linear relationship between the concentration of HSP70 and the electrochemical response value of DPV was obtained through mathematical modeling.

The sample was prepared by the method in the reference [38]. The adult blood was collected in the anticoagulant tube as the samples, which were centrifuged for 15 min at 1000 ×g at 2–8 °C within 30 min of collection. The supernatant was used as the tested samples, which were analyzed by the proposed electrochemical immunosensor and ELISA.

The morphology of graphene quantum dots (GQDs), polyaniline (PANI) and polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) were characterized by scanning electron microscopy (SEM). As shown in Fig. 1A, GQDs are small granular substances with a certain regular structure; PANI (Fig. 1B) shows the structure of nano-tubular, nano-sheets and nano-microspheres; when GQDs and PANI were combined, PAGD (Fig. 1C) presents a nanosolid tube morphology with a regular structure. Due to the fact that PANI tends to combine with GQDs in a longitudinal self-assembly fashion during the combination of GQDs and PANI, it forms a solid tube structure [37].

Fig. 1D is the comparison for characterization of preparing PAGD using Fourier infrared spectroscopy. From the curve b, it can be seen that the stretching vibration peak of the CO bond on the epoxy group of the GQDs appears at 1082.91 cm−1 and the vibration peak of the CH bond appears at 1399.95 cm−1. Absorption peaks at 1615.70 cm−1 and 1735.33 cm−1 correspond to vibrations of CC and CO respectively. The absorption peak appearing at 3408.91 cm−1 is attributed to the OH stretching vibration of the hydroxyl group on the structure of GODs. Comparing the infrared characterization results of GO in curve a, it can be found that the signal intensity of CC and CO bonds of GQDs increases, because more hydrophilic functional groups like epoxy and hydroxyl are formed on the surface of GQDs. Moreover, the appearance of these groups also makes the quantum dots more soluble in the aqueous phase. These results showed that the preparation of GQDs was successful. From the infrared characterization of PANI (curve c), it can be seen that 3264 cm−1 corresponds to the NH stretching vibration of the secondary amine group (NH), and the peak at 1581 cm−1 is the characteristic of the tensile deformation of the quinone diimidine ring, 1499 cm−1 is the characteristic of the CC vibration of the benzene ring; the peak at 1300 cm−1 is attributed to the stretching vibration of the aromatic amine (νC-N); 1143 cm−1 and 1035 cm−1 correspond to the CN stretching (N Aniline-N-) and CN stretching (N=chinone=N); 821 cm−1 is an out-of-plane bending vibration of aryl hydrogen (νC-H). The infrared characterization results of PAGD (curve d) show that PAGD almost possesses most of the absorption peaks of PANI and the peak moves. The experiment results show that PAGD composites had been successfully prepared. Due to the interaction of the OH group of COOH on GQDs with the secondary amine (NH) on PANI, νOH and νNH are weakened, resulting in the OH absorption peak of PAGD above 3200 cm−1 was weakened or even disappeared. Secondly, compared with PANI, the absorption peak of the quinone diimidine ring was shifted from 1582 to 1577 cm−1, a probable reason is the addition of GQDs, resulting in less rigid plutonium structure in PANI [39]. In addition, when PANI is immobilized on the GQDs plane, the aromatic structure in PANI is conjugated to GQDs through π-bonds [40], so that out-of-plane bending vibration of the aromatic hydrogens is limited, resulting in γ = CH moved from 824 to 822 cm−1. From the comparison of the infrared characterization results of GO, GQDs, PANI and PAGD, it can be known that the PAGD composite had been successfully prepared in this study.

Raman spectra are often used to detect the ordered or disordered crystal structure of carbon materials, where the G band is the first-order Raman scattering of the E2g optical mode, corresponding to the ordered Sp2 structure; the D band is the A1g phonon mode at the K-point of the Brillouin zone. It corresponds to the degree of disorder and the local defects at the edge. The intensity ratio of the D peak to the G peak (r = ID/IG) can be used to measure the degree of defects and confusion in the material. The D band is caused by the Sp2 to Sp3 hybridization, and this change affects the ordering [41]. It can be seen from the figure that the r-value of GO (0.51) is less than the r-value of GQDs (0.54), indicating that the ordering of GQDs is weakened and the Sp2 in the graphene oxide structure is converted into Sp3 during the preparation of GQDs. 1171, 1396, and 1462 cm−1 respectively correspond to the OH bending vibration, CN vibration, and CC stretching vibration of the benzene ring in PANI. After the composite of GQDs and PANI, these characteristic peaks move to 1178, 1398, and 1560 cm−1. The GQDs had been successfully attached to PANI, demonstrating that PAGD composite had been successfully prepared.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Electrochemical characterization of the prepared sensor
ce:section.ce:para: The process for fabrication of HSP70/PAGD/GCE was characterized by electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) and cyclic voltammetry (CV) in 5 mmol/L [Fe(CN)6]3−/4- solution (including 0.1 mol/L KCl). Compared with glassy carbon electrode (GCE) (a), GQDs/GCE (b) and PANI/GCE (c), the PAGD/GCE (d) shows a lower electron-transfer resistance (Ret), demonstrating the nanoparticle had improved reversibility of electron transfer process. Due to the exclusion action with a lot of COOH and OH groups of the GQDs, the smaller background current of GQD/GCE than the bare GCE indicates that GQDs with a lot of COOH and OH groups decreased the electrical conductivity. It was found that the background current increased and the semicircle portion at higher frequencies decreased, when the polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) were modified on the GCE indicates that PAGD can improve the electron transfer rate. The reason might be attributed to the excellent electrical conductivity of PAGD. Interestingly, modification of PAGD/GCE with HSP70 shows a significant increase in Ret (curve e). Fig. 2A, B show the Nyquist plots of bare GCE (a), GQDs/GCE (b), PANI/GCE (c), PAGD/GCE (d) and HSP70/PAGD/GCE (e) in 0.5 mM (Fe(CN)6)3−/4− (0.1 M KCl) solution, which were fitted by digital simulation based on Randles equivalent circuit (Fig. 2A, B inset). The electrolyte resistance (Rs), the double-layer capacitance (Cd), the Warburg impedance (Zw) and Rct are the four components of the Randles equivalent circuit. This phenomenon indicated to the poor conductivity of hydrophobic HSP70, which is reflected by the semicircular part of the spectrum, it can be concluded that HSP70 hinders the transmission of charge.

These results were further confirmed by cyclic voltammetry (CV) measurements for characterization of the HSP70/PAGD/GCE basic electrode. As shown in Fig. 2C, the background current of PAGD/GCE was larger than others, which illustrated that PAGD/GCE promoted the electron transfer rate on the surface of sensor. This phenomenon should be attributed to the interconnected multilayer network structure in which PAGD has an increased surface layer and improved electronic exchange efficiency. When the HSP70 was loaded on the surface of PAGD/GCE, the peak current determined by HSP70/PAGD/GCE has dramatically reduced due to the exclusion action with the hydrophobicity of HSP70. Based on the above results, it is a representation of the basic electrode was successfully fabricated.

Since the polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD) modified on the surface of the glassy carbon electrode (GCE) has amino group and is positively charged to some extent. So the carboxyl group of HSP70 (Ag) can dehydrate and condense with the amino group of PAGD. Meanwhile, fabricating the electrochemical immunosensor with the help of good biocompatibility and larger surface area of PAGD, ensures that HSP70 can firmly immobilize on the surface of PAGD/GCE through Van der Waals force and interionic forces. As shown in Fig. 2D, different modified electrodes were tested in the reaction liquid of immunoassay (0.1 M pH 7.4 PBS containing 2 mM o-phenylenediamine (o-PD) and 4 mM H2O2) using cyclic voltammetry (CV). When the HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE (Curve c) was used for measurement, a pair of stable and well defined redox peaks were observed at −0.632 V and −0.566 V, which corresponded to the redox of the enzyme products of 2, 3-diaminophenazine. By studying comparatively, it demonstrated that the immunosensor was fabricated successfully. With the using of PAGD, a large number of active sites was provided for immobilization of HSP70, and it also indirectly increased the loading amount of HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab in the enzymatic reaction.

Firstly, the different concentration of HSP70 was analyzed using Sandwich- ELISA. Standards are added to the appropriate micro ELISA plate wells and combined to the specific antibody. Then a biotinylated detection antibody specific for HSP70 and Avidin-Horseradish Peroxidase (HRP) conjugate was added to each micro plate well and incubated. Free components were washed away. The substrate solution was added to each well. Only those wells that contain HSP70, biotinylated detection antibody and Avidin-HRP conjugate will appear blue in color. Add 50 μL of stop solution to each well. Color turn to yellow immediately. Determine the optical density (OD value) of each well at once, using a microplate reader set to 450 nm. Fig. 3A shows the relationship between OD values and the different concentrations of HSP70. Fig. 3B is the calibration curve of the ELISA for HSP70 determination, which was plotted on a semi-log scale. The results of ELISA show a good linear calibration range from 0.85 to 60 ng/mL between the logarithm of HSP70 concentration and OD values. Linear equation was: OD = 1.169 LogCHSP70 (ng/mL) − 0.0117, R = 0.9595 with a detection limit (S/N = 3, 0.47 ng/mL) was obtained.

At the same time, the performance for analysis of human heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) was investigated using the constructed electrochemical immunosensor by differential pulse voltammetry (DPV). Due to the HSP70 fixed on the surface of basic electrode and the HSP70 in the samples can competitively combine with the horseradish peroxidase labeled HSP70 antibody (HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab). So, there is a negative relation between the biotin labeled HSP70 antibody (Biotin-Ab) contents combined with modified electrode and the HSP70 in the test solutions or the samples. Then the biotin labeled HSP70 antibody/HSP70/porous graphene modified glassy carbon electrode (Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE) reacted with horseradish peroxidase labeled streptavidin (HRP-Strept) to develop integrated HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab- HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrochemical immunosensor. Finally, the differential pulse voltammograms (DPV) scan was used to realize the sensitive determination of HSP70 in the test solution. It is easy to know that with the increase of HSP70 contents in the standard solution for test, the differential pulse response by HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrochemical immunosensor gradually decreased in the test liquid (0.1 M pH 7.4 PBS containing 2 mM o-PD and 4 mM H2O2) (Fig. 3C, D).

For comparison, the controlled experiment was performed as well. In this controlled experiment, immunosensors were made by the same way, except that there was no HSP70 in test solution. The controlled immunosensor showed a noticeably response peak in the test liquid (curve a). The reason why electrochemical detecting signal significantly increased using differential pulse voltammetry (DPV) was because of HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab combined with HSP70 on the surface of the basic electrode (HSP70/PAGD/GCE) fully, which increased the amount of HRP that was used to catalyze the conversion of o-phenylenediamine to 2, 3-phenazinediamine. However, the tested differential pulse response signal from solution which includes HSP70 antigen has apparently decreased compared with the controlled experiment. The effect of the conjugate concentration of HSP70 was investigated by testing the DPV response of different immunosensors which were prepared by incubating HSP70/PAGD/GCE in different concentration of HSP70 with same concentration of biotin labeled HSP70 antibody (Biotin-Ab). As shown in Fig. 3C, the current decreased with the increase of the concentration of HSP70 from 0 to 100 ng/mL. After more than 100 ng/mL, the peak current remained practically constant for larger conjugate concentrations. Therefore, 100 ng/mL probably corresponded to saturation of the HSP70 with Biotin-Ab. It indicated that the competitive immunosensor was successfully established, and it could be used for detecting HSP70 in biological samples. Additional, there is a negative correlation between the strength of differential pulse response signals and the concentrations of HSP70 in test solution. Moreover, the current response of the immunosensor was optimum when it was carried out at 37 °C for 30 min in incubation step.

Under the optimal condition, Fig. 3D shows the relationship between the response values of the DPV and different concentrations of HSP70 of standards analysis by immunosensor. Fig. 3E shows the calibration curve of the concentrations of HSP70 and the response values of DPV. It can be seen clearly that the differential pulse peak current of the immunosensor and the logarithm of HSP70's concentration exhibited a good linear relationship at the range of HSP70's concentration from 0.0976 ng/mL to 100 ng/mL. The liner equation is i(μA) = −6.7255 LogCHSP70(ng/mL) + 15.946 with the linearly dependent coefficient R2 of 0.9937 and the detection limit (S/N = 3) of 0.05 ng/mL.

Furthermore, the analytical performance of the proposed immunosensor has been compared with others which have been reported using for determination of HSP70. As shown in Table 1 , the fabricated immunosensor displays a better analysis performance, lower detection limit.

The components of biological products are extremely complex. Analyzing specific biological components always needs cockamamie sample preparation, while electrochemical immunosensor did not need sample separation to realize the sensitive detection of specific biological components. It showed that the electrochemical immunosensor had good specificity. This experiment set human heat shock protein 110 (HSP110), human heat shock protein 90 (HSP90), human heat shock protein 60 (HSP60) and human heat shock protein 28 (HSP28) as interferents to evaluate the specificity of prepared immunosensor. The concentration of HSP70 was 3.125 ng/mL, and the interferents were 100 ng/mL respectively (Fig. 4 ). According to the results, the recovery rates of HSP70 fluctuated from 100.97% to 105.38% (Table. S2) with the existence of these interferences, it indicated that high concentrations of interferents had no significant effect in the detection of HSP70. So, the competitive HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrochemical immunosensor based on the monoclonal anti human heat shock protein 70 antibody had great selectivity to HSP70.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Reproducibility and stability of the immunosensor
ce:section.ce:para: The reproducibility of HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE was also evaluated by detecting 3.125 ng/mL of HSP70 solution for five times using the same immunosensor, and detecting 3.125 ng/mL of HSP70 solution for five times using the three immunosensors fabricated with same method. The average relative standard deviations (RSD) of the intra-assay and inter-assay were 3.29% and 3.78%, respectively. It demonstrated that the relative standard deviations (RSD) of HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrochemical immunosensor's reproducibility were relatively small in the external experiment. The results indicated that the proposed immunosensor had acceptable reproducibility (Table. S1). Moreover, the prepared immunosensor was stored at 4 °C for 15 days and then evaluated the function of electrode to evaluate its long-term stability. Compared to its original current response, the immune sensor maintains 90.16% of its functionality, suggesting the acceptable stability of HSP70 immunosensor in detecting HSP70. The strong interaction between polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots (PAGD) and HSP70 ensured the good stability of the immunosensor. The reason why electrochemistry responses decrease slowly is the fixed biological molecule gradually inactivate.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Application of the immunosensor in human serum
ce:section.ce:para: The practical feasibility and applicability of ELISA and the fabricated immunosensor to detect HSP70 in several real clinical human serum samples was compared. The analysis process was tested three times parallel with the standard addition method as shown in Table. S3 and Fig. S4. The RSD of the proposed immunosensor ranged from 3.37% to 4.57% while the recovery rate changed between 98.89% and 101.71% in three independent experiments. The RSD of ELISA varied between 6.08% to 6.92% and the recovery rate altered from 94.78% to 104.89%. Therefore, it indicated that the results of proposed immunosensor were better than that of ELISA. Based on the good accuracy and reliability of the proposed method, the constructed HRP-Strept-Biotin-Ab-HSP70/PAGD/GCE electrochemical immunosensor can be used for determination of HSP70 in real clinical human serum samples which from the depression who was early diagnosed by scale evaluation and clinical judgment.

In summary, based on polyaniline functionalized graphene quantum dots composites (PAGD) with highly conductive, large specific surface area and optimized structure, a simple and sensitive electrochemical immunosensor for heat shock protein 70 (HSP70) detection was constructed successfully. The prepared PAGD exhibited many active sites and good biocompatibility which provided powerful conditions for loading HSP70 on the surface of electrode effectively. And the PAGD, as the signal amplifier, showed excellent electrical conductivity, leading to remarkable increase of electrochemical response signal. Moreover, based on the competitive immunity strategy, the response signal decreased with the increasing of HSP70 concentration. These results made this immunosensor achieve highly sensitive detection for HSP70 with low detection limit (0.05 ng/mL, S/N = 3) as well as a wide liner range of 0.0976–100 ng/mL. Meanwhile, the proposed electrochemical immunosensor exhibited good selectivity, satisfactory reproducibility and high stability, and successfully applied in real samples. Being compared with the commercial ELISA method under the same experimental conditions, the proposed method showed a superior performance. Therefore, this immunosensor can be envisaged as a useful and affordable analytical tool for the rapid determination of depression marker-HSP70 in clinical applications.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: The authors acknowledge the National Natural Science Foundation of China (81972018), National Key Research and Development Program of China (2018YFC1706300, 2018YFC17063003), Special Funds for Innovation and Guidance of Gansu (2017ZX-02), the Science-Technology plan Project from Lanzhou Science and Technology Bureau (2017-RC115), the Key R&D Project of Gansu Provincial Science and Technology Department (17ZD2FA009), the Key Project of the Fundamental Research Funds for the Central Universities (lzujbky-2018-k13, lzujbky-2017-205).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.msec.2020.110797.
